headline: NVIDIA Corp (NVDA.O): Computex Day 2: Capital Allocation, MRVL, & CPO
summary: NVIDIA hosted a financial analyst Q&A with Jensen Huang (CEO) at Computex 2026 (see day 1 takeaways). We also spoke with NVDA VP of Investor Relations & Strategic Finance, Toshiya Hari, post the financial analyst Q&A and recap our key takeaways below.

headline: NVIDIA Corp (NVDA.O): Computex Day 1 - All About CPUs - Positive
summary: Jensen Huang (CEO) delivered a GTC keynote on COMPUTEX Day 1. Our key incremental takeaways are: 1) Vera Rubin entered full production vs recent investor concerns around potential delays; 2) Nvidia announced its Vera agentic AI dedicated CPU with 3X performance on SQL workloads and 6X performance on data processing versus its X86 counterparts AMD/Intel. Moreover, CPU content seems close to 1 to 1 vs GPU. This is positive for CPUs as historically for 2 GPUs Nvidia sold 1 CPU. With agentic AI, Nvi

headline: NVIDIA Corp (NVDA.O): 10Q Tidbits
summary: We took a detailed look into Nvidia's 1QFY2027E 10Q and summarize our key takeaways. For more detailed analysis on the results and model update, please see our earnings review.

headline: NVIDIA Corp (NVDA.O): Positive CPU Commentary; Maintain Buy w/$300 TP
summary: NVDA reported better-than-expected Apr-Q results amid robust AI demand (see results). NVDA is adding more visibility to its Data Center sales by providing two sub-groups: Hyperscale and ACIE (AI Clouds, Industrial, & Enterprise) which we view as a positive for comparison purposes. NVDA expects $20B Vera CPU sales this year (includes Meta & MSFT we believe) and a new $200B CPU TAM by 2030. The prior >$1 trillion 2025-27’ DC sales didn’t include Vera CPUs and LPX contributions lifting our FY27/28/

headline: NVIDIA Corp (NVDA.O): Apr-Q Results Quick Take
summary: NVDA reported better-than-expected Apr-Q results amid robust AI demand strength (see details below). We are awaiting commentary on the earnings call at 5pm ET. See our Preview.

headline: NVIDIA Corp (NVDA.O): Preview – Maintain Buy w/$300 TP
summary: We model ~$1.4B upside in the Apr-Q with sales reaching $80B vs Street’s $78.6B on stronger-than-expected B300 ramp. Looking into the Jul-Q, we expect 11% Q/Q sales uptick to $89B vs Street $87B on continued ramp of B300 as reflected by the faster-than-expected 1.6T transceiver shipments. We model data center sales in Apr/Jul Qs to grow +18%/+11% Q/Q vs Street +15%/+10%. Using Citi’s updated hyperscale capex model of 83%/57% growth in CY26/27, we revise our FY27/28 EPS +4%/+10%. We maintain our

headline: NVIDIA Corp (NVDA.O): Takeaways from Quantum Day
summary: NVIDIA hosted a virtual Quantum Day with presentations from the quantum community and unveiled NVIDIA Ising, a family of open models for building better, more reliable qubits. NVIDIA included that Buy-rated INFQ (see our recent initiation report), one of its quantum partners, is already deploying and seeing the benefits of its Ising Decoding model.

headline: NVIDIA Corp (NVDA.O): GTC - Financial Analyst Q&A Takeaways
summary: Nvidia hosted financial analyst Q&A following Day 1 keynote (see Citi note here). We recap key takeaways. Maintain Buy.

headline: NVIDIA Corp (NVDA.O): GTC Keynote - Checks all the Boxes; Maintain Buy
summary: Jensen Huang (CEO) delivered GTC keynote today. Three key points jumped out to us: 1) NVDA is seeing an inflection in inference and expecting its DC sales to reach $1T in CY2025-2027 (in-line with Citi/investor expectations but above Street $950B) with tens of billions upside given $1T does not include LPU, CPU standalone, and Hopper sales. Moreover, with 9-12 months lead times 2027 projections can move higher: 2) 2026-2028 product roadmap showcasing NVDA’s extreme co-design with GPUs, CPUs as w

headline: NVIDIA Corp (NVDA.O): 10K Tidbits
summary: We took a detailed look into Nvidia's FY26 10-K and summarize our key takeaways. For more detailed analysis on the results and model update, please see our earnings note.

headline: NVIDIA Corp (NVDA.O): Raising TP to $300 into GTC; Maintain Buy
summary: NVDA stock was mostly flat after market despite guiding to an Apr-Q $5B beat and reaching $78B in sales on exponential agentic AI demand. We see GTC in mid-March as a positive event for the stock and expect Nvidia to showcase Groq SRAM low latency inference, CPUs, and Optical networking (see report). Moreover, early visibility into 2027 sales could catalyze stock.

headline: NVIDIA Corp (NVDA.O): Management Callback Notes
summary: We spoke with NVDA management post Jan-Q earnings and recap key takeaways.

headline: NVIDIA Corp (NVDA.O): Better-than-Expected Jan-Q Results & Apr-Q Guide Driven by Agentic AI
summary: NVDA is up 3% after-market following its better-than-expected Jan-Q results amid robust AI demand strength. We are awaiting commentary on the earnings call at 5pm ET. See our preview.

headline: US Communications Equipment: OFC Preview: Opening +ve CWs on LITE/GLW and neg CWs on ALAB/TEL
authors: Papa Sylla,Asiya Merchant, CFA,Atif Malik,Adrienne Colby
summary: Optical Fiber Communication (OFC) will be held in Los Angeles on March 17th – 19th. Among multiples trends, we expect OCS and CPO discussions to be front and center as NVIDIA and hyperscalers continue to push for optical networking adoption (see pages 5-11 on key optical trends). We raise our TPs and open positive Catalyst Watches (CWs) on LITE and GLW, which we consider to be pillars in AI optical networking ecosystem. While we understand that optics and copper-based networking can co-exist, we

headline: NVIDIA Corp (NVDA.O): Preview – Investors Looking Past Earnings to GTC 2026
summary: NVIDIA reports Jan-Q earnings on 2/25. We model Jan-Q sales of $67B above Street $65.6B and expect Apr-Q guide of $73B vs Street $71.6B. We expect continued strong ramp of B300 with Rubin launch to drive a 34% H/H acceleration in CY2H26 sales vs 27% in CY1H26. We believe most investors are looking past the earnings to annual GTC conference in mid-March for NVIDIA to talk about inference roadmap using Groq’s low latency SRAM IP and provide an early outlook for 2026/27 AI sales. We expect FY27 GM

headline: NVIDIA Corp (NVDA.O): CES Meeting Takeaways – Maintain Buy
summary: We attended NVIDIA CEO keynote/financial analyst Q&A and hosted CFO Colette Kress for an investor meeting at CES. Overall, we walked away positive on reasoning led agentic and physical AI demand inflection in 2026. We were surprised to see Vera Rubin details at CES and believe Rubin architecture will enhance/surpass Nvidia’s AI platform appeal vs Blackwell given increased architectural complexity with six new chips. The company sees upside to prior $500B AI 2025/26 AI demand from both Blackwell

headline: NVIDIA Corp (NVDA.O): Groq Deal: Doubling Down on the Inference Inflection
summary: Nvidia has announced an agreement to a nonexclusive licensing deal with prominent AI chip startup Groq for $20bn or ~3x the most recent valuation (Bloomberg, 12/24/25). Following the deal, Groq's founder and president will both leave the startup to join Nvidia. We view the deal as a clear positive on two fronts: (1) We think via this deal, which comes after Nvidia's Rubin CPX GPU announcement in September, Nvidia is indirectly acknowledging that specialized inference architectures are important

headline: NVIDIA Corp (NVDA.O): Next Gen AI Reasoning Models - a Boon for Inference & Memory Demand in 2026
summary: At re-Invent last week, AWS announced an episodic memory functionality to its Bedrock AgentCore platform to understand the context of a current interaction. We note this follows Google's Nested Learning approach to machine learning in early November and ambitions to double its AI compute capacity every six months. We believe this shift to massive context inference bodes well for both GPU/XPU and memory demand. Moreover, the two hyperscale updates follow Nvidia's Rubin CPX GPU announcement at AI

headline: NVIDIA Corp (NVDA.O): Updated Thoughts on TPU Competition
summary: Bloomberg reported today that Meta is in talks to use Google's TPU chips in 2027 following prior news that Meta might use Google's GCP cloud services next year (see our note from early September). We believe Custom AI accelerators, including the Google TPU and Amazon Trainium, continue to scale as these platforms increasingly support both internal AI workloads and external customers. Custom XPU expanding footprint provides Google and Amazon with cost advantages in AI infrastructure. That said, h
